projects.title: Unit 10 Project — Volume & Surface Area in Action
projects.desc: Apply volume and surface area to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 10.

projects.title: Volume Vault
projects.desc: Crack the vault by computing volumes of rectangular prisms (6.G.2).

projects.title: World Architect Math Project
projects.desc: Design structures and compute their volume in an open build challenge (6.G.2).

projects.title: Surface Area Packer
projects.desc: Unfold prisms into nets and pack the surface to find total area (6.G.4).

title: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
contentObjective: I can find the volume of a rectangular prism, including ones with fractional edge lengths, using base area × height.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words volume, rectangular prism, dimensions, and base area.

turnAndTalk.question: Your time capsule is 2 ft long, 1.5 ft wide, and 1 ft tall — one edge is a fraction. Does having a fractional edge change how you find the volume? Why or why not?
turnAndTalk.kernel: You still multiply length × width × height even when an edge is a fraction or decimal.

stems.en: A fractional edge ___ change the method because I still multiply ___ × ___ × ___.
stems.es: Una arista fraccionaria ___ cambia el método porque sigo multiplicando ___ × ___ × ___.

stems.en: The volume of the capsule is ___ ft³.
stems.es: El volumen de la cápsula es ___ ft³.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain the formula stays the same (V = l × w × h = 2 × 1.5 × 1 = 3 ft³) and that a fractional edge just means multiplying with a fraction or decimal.
turnAndTalk.extend: How could you use base area × height to find the same volume, and why does that give the same answer as l × w × h?
extendStems: Base area × height gives the same volume because base area is ___.
First I find the base area ___ × ___ = ___, then multiply by the height ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Capsule A (2 × 1.5 × 1) and Capsule B (3 × 1 × 1) both have a volume of 3 ft³. How did your multiplication produce the same volume from different dimensions?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Different dimensions can multiply to the same product, so the volumes match.

stems.en: Both capsules have V = ___ ft³ because ___ × ___ × ___ = ___ × ___ × ___.
stems.es: Ambas cápsulas tienen V = ___ ft³ porque ___ × ___ × ___ = ___ × ___ × ___.

stems.en: The dimensions are different but the ___ is the same.
stems.es: Las dimensiones son diferentes pero el ___ es el mismo.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students show 2 × 1.5 × 1 = 3 and 3 × 1 × 1 = 3, and explain that the same product means equal volume even with different shapes.
turnAndTalk.extend: If two capsules have the same volume but different dimensions, which would you choose to store long, flat objects, and why?
extendStems: I would choose Capsule ___ because its dimensions ___.
Same volume does not mean same usefulness because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A fish tank is 24 in long, 12 in wide, and 16 in tall, and one gallon takes about 231 cubic inches. Talk through how you would find how many gallons it holds.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Find the volume with V = l × w × h, then divide by 231 to convert to gallons.

stems.en: The tank's volume is ___ in³ because 24 × 12 × 16 = ___.
stems.es: El volumen del tanque es ___ in³ porque 24 × 12 × 16 = ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute 4608 in³, then divide by 231 to get about 20 gallons, and explain that dividing converts cubic inches into gallons.
turnAndTalk.extend: Why does the answer come out to about 20 gallons instead of an exact whole number, and how would you decide whether to round up or down for filling the tank?
extendStems: The answer is not exact because ___.
To fill the tank safely I would round ___ because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A classmate says a 5 × 3 × 2.5 m prism has volume 5 × 5.5 = 27.5 m³. How do you find and explain their mistake?
turnAndTalk.kernel: They added 3 + 2.5 instead of multiplying; the correct volume is 5 × 7.5 = 37.5 m³.

stems.en: The mistake is that they ___ instead of multiplying 3 × 2.5.
stems.es: El error es que ___ en lugar de multiplicar 3 × 2.5.

stems.en: The correct volume is ___ m³ because 3 × 2.5 = ___, then 5 × ___ = ___.
stems.es: El volumen correcto es ___ m³ porque 3 × 2.5 = ___, luego 5 × ___ = ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students identify the added-instead-of-multiplied error, compute 3 × 2.5 = 7.5, and conclude the correct volume is 37.5 m³.
turnAndTalk.extend: What general rule about the volume formula does this mistake break, and how could a quick estimate have caught it?
extendStems: The mistake breaks the rule that volume always uses ___.
An estimate would catch it because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Volume of Rectangular Prisms, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.

stems.en: Both capsules have V = ___ ft³ because ___ × ___ × ___ = ___ × ___ × ___.
stems.es: Ambas cápsulas tienen V = ___ ft³ porque ___ × ___ × ___ = ___ × ___ × ___.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.G.2 — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: How much space is inside a solid shape.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cuánto espacio hay dentro de una figura sólida.
vocabulary.visual: A box 2 × 1.5 × 1 = 3 ft³ — it holds 3 cubic feet of stuff
vocabulary.cloze: The amount of space inside a three-dimensional solid is its ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Số lượng đơn vị khối cần thiết để lấp đầy không gian ba chiều.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مقدار الفضاء ثلاثي الأبعاد الذي يشغله المجسم ويقاس بالوحدات المكعبة.

vocabulary.definition: A solid box shape with six flat rectangle sides.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura sólida en forma de caja con seis lados rectangulares.
vocabulary.visual: A tissue box, a fish tank, a brick — all rectangular prisms
vocabulary.cloze: A solid with six rectangular faces is a ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Hình hình học không gian có sáu mặt đều là hình chữ nhật.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مجسم ثلاثي الأبعاد جميع أوجهه الستة مستطيلات.

vocabulary.definition: The units used to measure space inside, like cubic inches.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Las unidades para medir el espacio interior, como pulgadas cúbicas.
vocabulary.visual: 1 ft³ = a cube that is 1 foot on every edge
vocabulary.cloze: The unit used to measure volume is ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Đơn vị dùng để đo thể tích (ví dụ: mét khối).
vocabulary.definitionAr: الوحدات المستخدمة لقياس الحجم (مثل: المتر المكعب).

vocabulary.definition: How long, how wide, and how tall a shape is.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan largo, ancho y alto es una figura.
vocabulary.visual: A box with dimensions 4 × 3 × 2 means l = 4, w = 3, h = 2
vocabulary.cloze: The measurements of length, width, and height of a solid are its ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Số đo chiều dài, chiều rộng hoặc chiều cao của một vật thể.
vocabulary.definitionAr: قياسات الطول أو العرض أو الارتفاع لجسم ما.

vocabulary.definition: A flat shape that folds up into a solid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura plana que se dobla y forma un sólido.
vocabulary.visual: Cut a cereal box along its edges, unfold it flat — that is the net
vocabulary.cloze: A flat pattern that folds into a solid is a ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Hình phẳng hai chiều có thể gấp lại thành hình học không gian.
vocabulary.definitionAr: شكل ثنائي الأبعاد يمكن طيه لتشكيل مجسم ثلاثي الأبعاد.

vocabulary.definition: The area of the bottom of a solid. Volume = base area × height.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El área del fondo de un sólido. Volumen = área de la base × altura.
vocabulary.visual: If the base is 6 × 4 = 24 cm² and h = 3, then V = 24 × 3 = 72 cm³
vocabulary.cloze: The area of the bottom face of a prism, multiplied by height to find volume, is the ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Diện tích của đa giác làm đáy cho một hình lăng trụ hoặc hình chóp.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مساحة المضلع الذي يمثل قاعدة المنشور أو الهرم.

launch.narrative: Your class is building a time capsule to bury on school grounds. The capsule is a rectangular prism that is 2 feet long, 1.5 feet wide, and 1 foot tall. You need to figure out how much stuff can fit inside — that means calculating the volume!
launch.contextImage: Students gathered around a rectangular box time capsule with items to place inside
noticePrompts: What are the three measurements of the time capsule?
What does volume measure — length, area, or space inside?
How is volume different from area?
wonderPrompts: What if we made the capsule taller but narrower — would the volume change?
What units do we use for volume versus area?

conceptIntro.heading: How do we find volume when an edge is a fraction?
conceptIntro.intro: Volume is the space inside a box. The rule V = length × width × height stays the same even when one edge is a fraction or decimal. You can also use volume = base area × height.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: My capsule is 2 ft long, 1.5 ft wide, and 1 ft tall. I write V = length × width × height.
I put in the numbers: V = 2 × 1.5 × 1.
So the volume is 3 ft³. A fractional edge like 1.5 did not change my steps at all.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now try a box that is 4 long, 2 wide, and 0.5 tall. What formula do we use?
Put in the numbers: V = 4 × 2 × 0.5. What is 4 × 2?
We got 8. Now multiply 8 × 0.5 (that means half of 8). What is the volume?
Yes, V = 4 cubic units. The decimal edge just made the answer a little smaller.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the table below, find the volume of each capsule design using V = length × width × height.
Some edges are fractions or decimals — use the same steps and write your answer in ft³.

explore.instructions: Calculate the volume of each possible time capsule design. Use V = l × w × h.

discourse.prompt: Capsules A and B have the same volume (3 ft³) but different dimensions. Why? Which design would you choose and why?
discourse.sentenceFrame: Capsules A and B both have V = ___ because ___ × ___ × ___ = ___ × ___ × ___. I'd choose ___ because ___.

optional.stem: A time-capsule box measures 6 in long, 4 in wide, and 3 in tall. What is its volume?
optional.explanation: V = length × width × height = 6 × 4 × 3 = 72 cubic in. Volume uses cubic units.

optional.stem: Two storage boxes are ready to pack. Box A is 5 × 5 × 2 inches and Box B is 4 × 4 × 3 inches. Which box holds more?
choices: Box A, because 50 cubic in is more than 48 cubic in
Box B, because 48 cubic in is more than 50 cubic in
Box B, because it is taller
optional.explanation: Box A: 5 × 5 × 2 = 50 cubic in. Box B: 4 × 4 × 3 = 48 cubic in. 50 > 48, so Box A holds more.

optional.stem: A keepsake box is 4 in long, 3 in wide, and 1½ in tall. What is its volume?
optional.explanation: V = 4 × 3 × 1.5 = 12 × 1.5 = 18 cubic in. A fractional edge does not change the steps.

optional.label: Match each box's dimensions to its correct volume.

optional.instructions: Sort each box by whether it holds MORE or LESS than the 60-cubic-inch time capsule.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Catch the Packing Mistake

workedExample.label: Dimensions
workedExample.work: A box is 7 in long, 3 in wide, and 2 in tall.

optional.correctWork: The student only multiplied length × width and forgot the height. V = 7 × 3 × 2 = 21 × 2 = 42 cubic in.
hints: Check Step 3: did the student use all three dimensions?
Volume needs length × width × height. Multiply 21 by the height of 2 to get 42 cubic in.

approaching.stem: What is the volume of a rectangular prism with l = 4 in, w = 3 in, h = 5 in?
approaching.explanation: V = l × w × h = 4 × 3 × 5 = 60 cubic inches.

approaching.stem: A box has a volume of 36 cm³. Its length is 6 cm and width is 3 cm. What is the height?
How is volume different from area?
choices: Volume measures 3D space (cubic units); area measures 2D surface (square units)
Volume uses square units; area uses cubic units
Volume only applies to cubes; area applies to all shapes
approaching.explanation: Volume measures the space inside a 3D shape (cubic units like in³). Area measures a flat 2D surface (square units like in²).

approaching.instructions: Find the volume of each rectangular prism.

approaching.label: Sort these boxes from SMALLEST volume to LARGEST volume.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Volume of Rectangular Prisms.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.

onLevel.label: A shipping container is 8 ft long, 4 ft wide, and 3 ft tall. It is half full of boxes.
onLevel.questionText: The container is half full. How many cubic feet of space are left?

onLevel.instructions: Find the missing dimension or volume for each container.

onLevel.stem: Two boxes: Box A is 10 × 5 × 4 inches. Box B is 8 × 6 × 5 inches. Which has more volume and by how much?
onLevel.explanation: Box A: 10 × 5 × 4 = 200 in³. Box B: 8 × 6 × 5 = 240 in³. Box B is bigger by 40 in³.

onLevel.label: A pool is 12 ft × 8 ft × 5 ft. Sort the statements from FALSE to TRUE.
items: FALSE: The pool volume is 96 ft³
TRUE: The pool volume is 480 ft³
correctOrder: FALSE: The pool volume is 96 ft³
TRUE: The pool volume is 480 ft³

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Volume of Rectangular Prisms.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height.

extending.title: Find the Volume Error
hints: 3 × 2.5 = 7.5, not 5.5. The student may have added instead of multiplied.

extending.prompt: A toy company ships action figures in boxes that are 4 × 3 × 6 inches. A shipping crate is 24 × 12 × 18 inches. How many action figure boxes fit in the crate? Explain your reasoning step by step.
extending.sentenceFrame: Each toy box has a volume of ___ in³. The crate has a volume of ___ in³. I can fit ___ boxes because ___. I checked by ___.

extending.instructions: Two L-shaped containers are each made from two rectangular prisms joined together. Find the total volume of each.

optionalActivity.name: Package Volume Design Lab
optionalActivity.intro: Step into the time-capsule packing lab and design boxes that hold exactly the right amount.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Volume of Rectangular Prisms is skipping the key idea: "A fractional edge does not change the rule: still multiply length × width × height." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: An aquarium store sells fish tanks. A small tank is 24 inches long, 12 inches wide, and 16 inches tall. One gallon of water takes up about 231 cubic inches.
connect.promptQuestion: How many gallons of water does the small tank hold?
connect.prompt: The tank's volume is ___ in³ because V = ___ × ___ × ___. It holds about ___ gallons because ___ ÷ 231 ≈ ___.

exitTicket.stem: A rectangular prism has l = 7 ft, w = 2 ft, h = 3 ft. What is the volume?
exitTicket.explanation: V = 7 × 2 × 3 = 42 cubic feet. Remember: volume uses cubic units (ft³).

printables.name: Fractional Edge Volume Boxes
printables.desc: Students find the volume of prisms with fractional edges using base area × height.

printables.name: Volume of Rectangular Prisms — Color by Number
printables.desc: Solve each problem, then color by the answer to reveal a picture.

printables.name: Volume of Rectangular Prisms — Vocabulary Word Search
printables.desc: Find this lesson's key vocabulary terms in the puzzle.
